questions.q: Baudhayana is best known as the author of which set of ancient Indian texts?
questions.explanation: Baudhayana composed the Baudhayana Sulba Sutra, the oldest of the surviving Sulba Sutras.

questions.q: The Sulba Sutras are primarily concerned with the geometry of constructing what?
questions.explanation: The Sulba Sutras give rules for building Vedic fire altars (vedis) of precise shapes and areas.

questions.q: A statement equivalent to which famous geometric theorem appears in Baudhayana's Sulba Sutra?
questions.explanation: Baudhayana stated a form of what is now called the Pythagorean theorem, centuries before Pythagoras.

questions.q: The word 'sulba' (or 'shulba') in 'Sulba Sutras' refers to what?
questions.explanation: 'Sulba' means cord or rope, the measuring tool used to lay out the altars geometrically.

questions.q: Baudhayana gave an accurate approximation for the square root of which number?
questions.explanation: Baudhayana provided a remarkably accurate approximation of the square root of two.

questions.q: In what broad civilization and tradition did Baudhayana work?
questions.explanation: Baudhayana belonged to the Vedic tradition of ancient India and wrote in Sanskrit.

questions.q: The Sulba Sutras are considered part of which larger body of ritual literature?
questions.explanation: The Sulba Sutras form part of the Kalpa, a Vedanga concerned with ritual procedure.

questions.q: Baudhayana's work is written in which language?
questions.explanation: The Sulba Sutras were composed in Sanskrit, the language of Vedic ritual texts.

questions.q: Baudhayana is regarded today primarily as an early figure in which field?
questions.explanation: He is celebrated as one of the earliest mathematicians and geometers of ancient India.

questions.q: Baudhayana lived and wrote roughly during which broad period?
questions.explanation: Baudhayana is generally dated to the first millennium BCE, well before the Common Era.

questions.q: Baudhayana's Sulba Sutra is generally considered to be which among the Sulba Sutras?
questions.explanation: The Baudhayana Sulba Sutra is the oldest of the surviving Sulba Sutra texts.

questions.q: Which of these later authors also composed a Sulba Sutra, after Baudhayana?
questions.explanation: Apastamba, along with Katyayana and Manava, composed later Sulba Sutras.

questions.q: Baudhayana's rule for the diagonal of a rectangle effectively states that the square on the diagonal equals what?
options: The sum of the squares on the two sides
Twice the product of the sides
The square on the longer side only
Half the sum of the sides
questions.explanation: He stated that the diagonal's square equals the sum of the squares on the length and breadth, the Pythagorean relation.

questions.q: Approximately how accurate is Baudhayana's value for the square root of two?
options: Correct to about five decimal places
Expressed only as a fraction one-half
questions.explanation: His approximation matches the true value of root two to about five decimal places, a striking accuracy.

questions.q: Why did Vedic priests need precise geometric rules like those in the Sulba Sutras?
options: To build altars of exact shapes and areas for rituals
questions.explanation: Ritual required altars of specific shapes and exact areas, demanding accurate geometric construction.

questions.q: Which everyday geometric task is addressed by the Sulba Sutras?
options: Constructing a square equal in area to a given circle
questions.explanation: The Sulba Sutras give methods to 'square the circle' approximately, converting between square and circular altars.

questions.q: Baudhayana's geometric knowledge of the right-triangle relation predates the Greek mathematician Pythagoras by roughly how long?
questions.explanation: Baudhayana's statement predates Pythagoras by several centuries, given his first-millennium-BCE dating.

questions.q: The literary style of the Sulba Sutras, like other sutras, is best described as which?
questions.explanation: Sutra literature uses terse, aphoristic statements meant to be memorized and expanded by commentary.

questions.q: Which shape transformation problem is characteristic of the Sulba Sutras' altar geometry?
options: Folding a cube into a cylinder
Converting a rectangle into a square of equal area
Twisting a triangle into a spiral
questions.explanation: The texts describe transforming a rectangle into a square of equal area, a core area-preservation problem.

questions.q: Besides the Sulba Sutra on geometry, the broader Baudhayana corpus also includes texts on what?
options: Ritual and religious law (Dharma and Srauta sutras)
questions.explanation: The Baudhayana tradition also produced Srauta, Grihya and Dharma sutras dealing with ritual and law.

questions.q: Which statement about the role of the Pythagorean-type rule in Baudhayana's text is correct?
options: It was used only as abstract philosophy
It was applied practically to lay out perpendicular sides and diagonals of altars
It was used to weigh gold
questions.explanation: The rule was applied practically to construct right angles and correct diagonals for altar layouts.

questions.q: What does the term 'sutra' indicate about the form of Baudhayana's writings?
options: They are threads of compact, rule-like statements
questions.explanation: 'Sutra' literally means thread, denoting a compact rule that threads together ritual or geometric instruction.

questions.q: Baudhayana's approximation of the square root of two is expressed as a sum of unit fractions. Which expression matches it?
questions.explanation: His rule gives root two as 1 + 1/3 + 1/(3·4) − 1/(3·4·34), accurate to about five decimals.

questions.q: Which of these Pythagorean triples is among those that appear or are usable in the Sulba Sutra constructions?
questions.explanation: The (3, 4, 5) right triangle is a classic triple consistent with the Sulba Sutra diagonal rule.

questions.q: The Baudhayana Sulba Sutra is attached as an appendix to which Srauta tradition?
questions.explanation: The Baudhayana Sulba Sutra belongs to the Taittiriya school of the Krishna (Black) Yajurveda.

questions.q: Which famous fire-altar of complex shape required especially advanced geometry described in the Sulba tradition?
questions.explanation: The falcon-shaped syena altar, built from many bricks to an exact area, demanded sophisticated geometric construction.

questions.q: When the area of an altar had to be increased while keeping its shape, the Sulba Sutras effectively scaled lengths by what factor for a doubling of area?
options: By the square root of two
questions.explanation: Doubling the area while preserving shape requires scaling each linear dimension by the square root of two, motivating that approximation.

questions.q: How do scholars approximately date the Baudhayana Sulba Sutra?
questions.explanation: Most scholars place the Baudhayana Sulba Sutra around the 8th to 5th centuries BCE.

questions.q: Which limitation best characterizes the Sulba Sutras' treatment of results like the square root of two and circle-squaring?
options: They give formal proofs in the Greek axiomatic style
They give practical construction rules and approximations without formal proofs
questions.explanation: The Sulba Sutras present practical rules and approximations rather than formal axiomatic proofs.

questions.q: What is the historical significance of Baudhayana's diagonal rule for the history of mathematics?
options: It is the earliest known calculus text
It is among the earliest known statements of the Pythagorean relationship
It introduced the decimal place-value system
It first defined the number zero
questions.explanation: Baudhayana's rule is one of the earliest known statements of the Pythagorean relationship, predating Pythagoras.
